event_blocks.summary: In a modern kitchen, Ed O’Neill, wearing a dark sweater and collared shirt, enters smiling as Sofía Vergara, pregnant and adorned in a blue wrap dress, large hoop earrings, and a silver bracelet, inquires about grapes near the counter. Ed deadpans that he ate them, leading Sofía to question his consumption of the New Year's grapes, and she passionately explains the tradition of eating twelve grapes for twelve wishes, recounting her personal journey from single motherhood to prosperity, asserting her right to eat the grapes, while Ed skeptically questions the tradition's efficacy. Transitioning to an interview setting, Ed O’Neill, identified by a text overlay, addresses the camera, detailing plans to take Phil, Claire, Mitch, and Cam to a Palm Springs hotel for New Year's Eve, including dinner, dancing, and a midnight toast. The scene then shifts to a warmly lit dining area with red curtains and a checkered rug, where Phil Dunphy, in a light blue long-sleeve shirt, animatedly describes an impressive telekinesis video to Claire Dunphy, who has curly blonde hair and wears a light blue shirt. Phil gestures expressively, recounting his attempt to levitate bacon, to which Claire, leaning on the table and listening intently with slight frowns and nods, responds with skepticism and sarcasm, questioning his work obligations and suggesting his goal is improbable with the idiom 'when pigs fly'.

The Pilot clarifies, "Well, first of all, that lady is my son," leading to a complex exchange about identity and relationships as the Man in the Striped Shirt learns the woman in question is the Pilot's wife, culminating in the Pilot's assertive, "You got a problem with her, you got a problem with me."

event_blocks.summary: At a nighttime party featuring string lights and a purple arch, the man in suit (party) urgently tells Gloria, the woman in beige, "No, no, no, Jay," warning him his "problem's about to get a lot bigger," while the man in navy sweater observes with a smile. Gloria pleads with Jay, stating, "No, Jay, I know you think this is what I want, but it's not," and admits, "I was just being stupid today. I like that you're calm... I need that in my life," prompting the man in suit (party) to reassure her, "I got this, Gloria." After another man tells someone to "walk away, grandpa," Gloria shouts, "Nobody! Go get him, grandpa!" Angered, the man in suit (party) ends the party, declaring, "Okay, that's it. No boat. Everybody off my dock, I'm calling the cops." The scene then shifts to a daytime suburban street where the sound of a motorcycle engine is heard. The Man in Leather Jacket, the biker, rides up to a couple, the man in dark shirt and the woman in purple blouse, who look surprised. The man in dark shirt advises, "be careful, I don't want you to crash," and expresses concern about scratching the bike, while the woman in purple blouse warns, "don't be nervous. People make mistakes when they're nervous." The biker confidently retorts, "I'm not nervous. I've had bigger hogs than this between my legs," before accelerating away, thinking, "He really ought to run things through his head first," and admitting internally, "I know, I looked super cool on the outside. But on the inside..."

He speaks about turning 50, vowing to step out of his comfort zone, and thanks his family for being his "biggest cheerleaders," adding "I love you too, honey." The attentive audience includes a young woman in a white top and a pregnant woman. The scene is briefly interrupted by rapid, comedic dialogue about "Haley's going into labor," apologies, a voice saying "convenient," and sounds of knocking and a door. Philip then begins to play a complex, classical piano piece. As he finishes, the audience claps and cheers, with voices praising "Great work, honey" and saying, "Tell your mom I say hi." The atmosphere abruptly shifts to a modern kitchen setting where Sofía Vergara, pregnant and wearing a blue wrap dress and black leather jacket, enters smiling warmly and cradling her belly, followed by a young boy in a blue t-shirt. She is greeted by others, including a man in a gray shirt and a woman in a black top, as a woman's voice comments, "Oh gross, they're at it again," marking a transition from a formal recital to a casual family gathering.

Not really gaining weight the way I would have expected," while holding a phone and reacting to the entrance of Sofía Vergara, a pregnant woman in a blue wrap dress and black leather jacket, who cradles her belly as she walks in, followed by a young boy in a blue t-shirt. The man in the gray shirt then adds, "I finally understand why people say pregnant women glow." The setting is warm and decorated with framed art and a zebra-print chair. An off-screen woman addresses him, saying "Phil, I've had three children." Phil continues to speak humorously about low expectations for birthdays, mentioning past disappointments like not getting an iPad or a surprise party at batting cages, and making a self-deprecating remark about "Jeff Sweeney taking a fastball to the plums." The camera zooms in on his face as he continues talking. The scene shifts again to a hallway near a staircase, filled with general party chatter and the distinct sound of something falling. A young boy in an orange shirt, identified as Manny, reports to an adult, "Hey, um, Dad, your cake fell off the counter, but Uncle Cam's trying to fix it." We got something to celebrate right now." On-screen text appears: 'STICKER PACK LINK IN BIO' with a small graphic of stickers. The atmosphere is cheerful and familial, culminating in multiple voices singing "Happy Birthday, Phil!" and "Happy, happy!", suggesting a baby shower or birthday event for Phil.

event_blocks.summary: In a decorated living room filled with balloons and flowers, Phil Dunphy, wearing a navy sweater, enters smelling baked brie and playfully sits on a striped couch to read 'The Art of War,' exclaiming to Cam about an incident. As a baby is seen in the background with people descending stairs, a chorus sings 'Happy Birthday Phil!', to which Phil responds about getting in trouble for fighting and saving a marriage, eliciting affirmations of it being the best day ever. The scene shifts to Sofía Vergara playing chess with a man with short gray hair, a young boy in an orange shirt observing; Sofía exclaims and laughs, then, after the man's comment about queens and champions, she makes a move, stands abruptly, and declares she'll burn the house down if she loses, prompting the man to de-escalate by noting they both lose if one wins and the other feels lousy. A loud crash is heard, followed by Phil's comment about his birthday present, and the man admits Sofía had him, that he couldn't win. A young girl in a denim vest and red shirt, holding a blue toy in a car, looks out at a green landscape, asking 'What you got?' and receiving the answer 'Hurricane Harriet,' signaling a transition. Inside a dimly lit bus, the young girl in the denim vest over a red shirt, holding a blue 'Happy' folder, describes 'Harriet' as a ditzy, problematic character to a woman in a black leather jacket, who finds it unrealistic. A woman in a dark blue dress confidently walks down the aisle, smiling and interacting with a male bus attendant, asking about the bathroom and then her ticket, which she says is in her seat, leading the attendant to sternly tell her to 'go back where you came from,' a phrase she mockingly repeats, her playful reflection seen in a window with a green landscape outside. A loud, frustrated female voice exclaims, 'They have to be thick out there!' The young girl's voice returns, stating Harriet 'just sent those people right into a bear cave' and 'never knows what she's doing,' with the woman in the leather jacket adding, 'What an idiot!' The segment transitions to an outdoor shot of a man in a black leather jacket looking up at the sun, his face illuminated. On-screen text lists executive and co-executive producers. The minute concludes with a distressed male voice pleading for help.

The man playing chess with Sofía calmly tells her "Easy," then adds, "You can't sing 'We are the champions' without your queen," as Sofía makes a move, stands up abruptly, and gestures animatedly, possibly in frustration. This is ridiculous. One of us is going to win the game, the other's going to feel lousy and we both lose." A loud crash is heard, possibly from Sofía's frustration, followed by a man's voice (likely Phil) commenting, "Yeah, just leave my birthday present on the floor." event_blocks.summary: Under a bright blue sky with a strong sun flare, the Man in Black Leather Jacket (outdoor), with short, dark, slightly graying hair, initially exclaims, "I don't want to die!" in a distressed tone, then shifts to a calm, conversational voice, contemplating a playlist for being trapped under a motorcycle, listing songs like "Stuck in the Middle," "Hit the Road Jack," "Heavy D," and "Pointer Sisters Neutron Dance." On-screen text overlays appear, displaying '@modernfamilyshow' and 'STICKER PACK LINK IN BIO'. He then sarcastically reflects, "Turns out today wasn't the first day of the rest of my life," before sighing and, sounding surprised then dismissive, instructs to "throw the whole box away" of DVDs he was holding for a buddy. Raising his right hand to shield his forehead from the sun as rolling end credits appear, he engages in rhythmic beatboxing with "Boots 'n' cats" sounds and high-pitched vocalizations. Wearily stating, "I'm so hungry. I don't know how long I'm going to be out here. I gotta ration my food," he unwraps and loudly chews a sandwich. Finally, in a frustrated, comedic outburst, he emphatically declares, "I said no tomatoes!"

event_blocks.summary: In a modern kitchen, Phil Dunphy attempts to mediate a dispute between two young boys, one in a black shirt and the other in a green shirt holding a football, by suggesting their argument is silly. The boy elaborates that he teased his sibling for eating the same lunch daily and for his mother being a "coal digger," a statement that prompts Phil to suggest moving on. Sofía, questioning who called her a coal digger, is told by the boy, "That's what my mom told me." Claire Dunphy, in a dark cardigan, asks Phil what a coal digger is, and he corrects her, saying it's "gold digger." Sofía denies ever saying she was a coal digger, but the boys insist she said it in the car, at Christmas, and at a Mexican restaurant. Claire then dismisses the boy, noting his own forgetfulness about leaving sweatshirts at school, before Sofía asks her a question and Claire begins to speak to Gloria. raw_segments: The video opens in a modern, well-lit kitchen where a group of people are gathered around a white kitchen island, creating a warm, familial atmosphere. Phil Dunphy, in a blue and black striped polo shirt, places his hand on the shoulder of a young boy in a black shirt, then moves to another young boy in a green shirt holding a football, while his voice is heard suggesting, "But it seems kind of silly now, what we're even fighting about, huh?" A young boy's voice then states, "I made fun of his accent," prompting Sofía Vergara, in a black top with sequined shoulders, to exclaim with a distinct accent, "What accent!" The boy continues, "I made fun of him for having the same thing for lunch every day," followed by laughter, and then adds, "I made fun of him because his mom used to dig coal." After a male voice asks "What?", the boy clarifies, "He said you were a coal digger." Phil's voice interjects, "Okay, I think we can move on." Sofía's voice, with her accent, asks, "Who said I was a coal digger?" The boy responds, "That's what my mom told me." Claire Dunphy, in a dark cardigan, is seen speaking animatedly to a man in a gray shirt, and her voice asks, "What's a coal digger?" Phil's voice clarifies, "Sweetheart, you heard it wrong. It's gold digger." Claire's voice then says, "I'm gonna have to call you back." Sofía's voice denies, "Well, I really do not think that I remember ever saying that." The young boy's voice lists, "Well, you said in the car, you said at Christmas," and another young boy's voice adds, "You said in the Mexican restaurant." Claire's voice quickly responds, "Okay, mister, leaves his sweatshirts at school every day, suddenly remembers everything. Thank you." Sofía's voice asks, "Honey, what's all in my head, huh?" before Claire's voice begins, "Oh, listen, Gloria, it it was like..." The scene then transitions to a red promotional graphic with white text reading '7 ORIGINAL STICKERS' and 'JUST FOR LIMITED TIME', showcasing various humorous stickers with quotes and images, including one of a man holding a book, a woman shouting 'JAAAAAAY!!!', and a Batman-themed sticker. These stickers are shown applied to a laptop, mug, and phone case, emphasizing their versatility. Throughout this promotional segment, upbeat, dramatic orchestral music with a driving rhythm plays, shifting the overall tone from a domestic family moment to a commercial promotion.
